attempts.insight: Mathlib's Module.Finite.equiv : (M ≃ₗ[R] N) → [Module.Finite R M] → Module.Finite R N consumes the hypothesis on the LHS of the equiv and produces the conclusion on the RHS. Iter-035's HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv has signature HModule' k F n X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n — LHS = X₄ form (which is the hypothesis side downstream), RHS = global HModule (which is the conclusion side). No .symm needed (contrast with iter-036's finrank corollary which did need .symm to flip orientation). Pure term-mode one-liner; no by-block, no rewrite, no Mathlib search past Module.Finite.equiv itself.
